description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a rounded, bulging midsection and flat top and bottom surfaces. The body is constructed from vertical wooden staves, each with a slightly faceted appearance, and is held together by two prominent dark metal hoops—one near the top and one near the bottom. The wood displays a rich, medium-brown color with visible grain patterns and some darker patches, suggesting age or use. The metal bands are matte and slightly reflective, providing structural reinforcement and a visual contrast to the wood. There are no visible handles, spigots, or markings, and the top surface appears to be a solid, closed lid. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban environments such as alleys, storage areas, or near shops and restaurants.
description: A medium-sized, classic wooden barrel with dark metal hoops and a closed, flat top. The wood is brown with visible grain and some darker patches, giving it a weathered look.
description_view_0: The barrel is seen from a slightly angled side, showing the rounded wooden staves and both metal hoops clearly, with the top surface fully visible.
description_view_1: This view highlights a darker patch on the wood near the upper metal hoop, with the barrel's curvature and banding prominent.
description_view_2: The barrel appears more uniformly colored from this angle, with subtle wood grain and both hoops visible.
description_view_3: A side view showing a mix of lighter and darker wood tones, with the metal hoops and the bulging shape of the barrel evident.
